title: Year 7 Mathematics: Indigenous Perspectives Discussion - Interactive Learning Experience
subject: Mathematics
culturalContext: Connecting mathematical activities to the local environment of Mangakōtukutuku College and Ngāti Kahungunu traditions through hands-on, practical applications.
learningObjectives: Engage in hands-on mathematics activities through cultural and local context
Apply traditional knowledge and modern methods to mathematics challenges
Connect learning to local environment, community, and cultural heritage
Demonstrate respect for Māori and Pacific perspectives and values
Use Te Reo Māori appropriately in mathematics contexts
activities: Traditional knowledge workshop and mathematics integration
Community interview project on mathematics applications
Cultural artifact study through mathematics lens
resources: Local kaumātua for traditional measurement knowledge
Cultural measurement references and traditional methods

tasks: Formative: Teacher observation during activities, discussions, and collaborative work
Formative: Peer feedback and self-reflection on learning progress
Summative: Student presentation demonstrating understanding, cultural connection, and community application
Summative: Portfolio of work showing growth in knowledge, skills, and cultural understanding

nzcAlignment: Mathematics and Statistics: Number and Algebra (Level 4-5)
Mathematics and Statistics: Geometry and Measurement (Level 4-5)
Te Tiriti o Waitangi: Kaitiakitanga and environmental stewardship

activitySteps.title: Whakawhanaungatanga: Building Connections
activitySteps.description: Begin with karakia and introductions. Students share their prior knowledge, experiences, and personal connections to the topic. Establish cultural protocols and respectful learning environment.

activitySteps.title: Cultural Context Exploration
activitySteps.description: Students will engage in traditional Māori measurement techniques alongside modern mathematical tools, connecting to local landmarks and cultural significance through interactive exploration.
materials: Local kaumātua for traditional measurement knowledge
Cultural measurement references and traditional methods

activitySteps.title: Hands-on Learning and Application
activitySteps.description: Practical application of mathematics concepts using both traditional and modern methods. Students work collaboratively to solve real-world problems connected to their local community.

activitySteps.title: Reflection and Cultural Connection
activitySteps.description: Students reflect on their learning journey, connecting new knowledge to cultural values, community needs, and personal growth. Share insights and celebrate achievements.

differentiation.support: Provide additional scaffolding, visual aids, and one-on-one support for students who need extra help
differentiation.extension: Offer advanced challenges, independent research opportunities, and leadership roles for students who excel
differentiation.cultural: Ensure all students can connect to their own cultural backgrounds while learning about Māori perspectives

safety.considerations: Follow all school safety protocols, ensure cultural protocols are respected, and provide appropriate supervision
safety.equipment: Check all equipment before use, ensure proper handling of cultural materials, and maintain respectful environment
